summary: Verified frontend gracefully handles zero-workspaces state after admin wipe. All UI flows pass: topbar shows 'No workspaces' label, dropdown shows EmptyState with CTA linking to /app/settings/workspaces, Workspaces page shows EmptyState with '+ New workspace' button, creating 'QA Test Depot' works (toast + 1 row), dashboard topbar updates to 'All workspaces', and /app/swms + /app/hazards capture pages load cleanly with the recreated workspace. No JS / console errors observed (only React DevTools info notice). Cleanup via UI failed by design: backend safety guard refuses to soft-delete the last workspace ('Cannot delete the only workspace — create another one first' toast/400). The original wipe was done via direct Mongo writes from /app/backend/wipe_seed_workspaces.py, so main agent must re-run that script (or direct-Mongo delete) if a zero-workspace post-state is required.
action_items: Re-run /app/backend/wipe_seed_workspaces.py (or equivalent direct-Mongo delete) to soft-delete the leftover 'QA Test Depot' workspace — the API cannot delete the last remaining workspace by design.
critical_code_review_comments: Workspaces.jsx load() does not await isAdmin->users when admin call fails partially; minor — uses .catch fallback so it's safe.
AppShell TopBar refetches /api/workspaces on every location.pathname change which is cheap but could be optimised via the WorkspaceProvider; acceptable for current scope.
seed_data_creation: Created and (attempted to) delete 'QA Test Depot' workspace. It remains active because backend forbids deleting the only workspace.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zero-workspace topbar/empty-state flow verified end-to-end with admin@paneltec.com. The DB currently contains 1 active workspace (QA Test Depot) because backend refuses to delete the last one — main agent should run wipe_seed_workspaces.py if zero-state is required again.
rca of the issue: UI cleanup is blocked by intentional backend rule in /app/backend/workspaces.py (refuses delete when count == 1). Reproduction: with only one active workspace in the org, click the row Delete button → confirm → API returns 400 with message 'Cannot delete the only workspace — create another one first'. Mitigation: run the existing wipe script /app/backend/wipe_seed_workspaces.py which performs direct Mongo soft-delete and bypasses the safety guard.
